title: Warhammer Quest - Take a Look - Part 11 - The White Dwarf Issues (c)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G76WtaecxB4

Daniel (@dungeondive) continues the exhaustive Warhammer Quest series with more White Dwarf magazine coverage, starting with a missed gem from issue 191 — a three-mission mini campaign called "A Horror Awakens" by Gavin Thorpe using Catacombs of Terror components.

Issue 192 proves to be a standout. It includes a cardboard supplement with new tiles — a collapsed passage creating bottleneck corridors, a dead-end with secret passage rules, and a stairwell allowing multi-level dungeon exploration. The accompanying article "Into the Depths" by Gavin Thorpe and Andy Jones explains how to integrate these pieces. The issue also features a remarkable interview with artist John Blanche and his distinctive sketchy, atmospheric style that helped define the Warhammer aesthetic.

Issue 194 offers a party composition guide by game creator Andy Jones, rating each warrior's fighting, magic, and healing abilities — the Warrior Priest earning a glowing verdict as a versatile party addition. Issue 195 contains the Domain of the Horned Rat, a Skaven-themed expansion Daniel suspects was originally planned as a full boxed set. It includes comprehensive Skaven event decks, rules for warpstone and Rat Golems, a large objective room tile with the Warp Fire Generator, and three complete adventures.

----

Which White Dwarf-era expansion would you most want to see given the full boxed treatment?
